columns.extraction_plan: Extract the value from the "Gleason Score" row, "≤7" sub-row, under the "Abiraterone trial | Standard of care plus abiraterone (n=501)" column, which is 115 (23%). Also extract the value from the "Gleason Score" row, "≤7" sub-row, under the "Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial | Standard of care plus enzalutamide and abiraterone (n=462)" column, which is 79 (17%).

Extract the value from the "Gleason Score" row, "≤7" sub-row, under the "Abiraterone trial | Standard of care (n=502)" column, which is 118 (24%). Also extract the value from the "Gleason Score" row, "≤7" sub-row, under the "Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial | Standard of care (n=454)" column, which is 92 (20%).

For the "Abiraterone trial | Standard of care plus abiraterone (n=501)" column, sum the counts and percentages for Gleason scores "8", "9", and "10". This results in 117+231+18 = 366 (23%+46%+4% = 73%). For the "Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial | Standard of care plus enzalutamide and abiraterone (n=462)" column, sum the counts and percentages for Gleason scores "8", "9", and "10". This results in 94+258+17 = 369 (20%+56%+4% = 80%).

For the "Abiraterone trial | Standard of care (n=502)" column, sum the counts and percentages for Gleason scores "8", "9", and "10". This results in 106+245+23 = 374 (21%+49%+5% = 75%). For the "Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial | Standard of care (n=454)" column, sum the counts and percentages for Gleason scores "8", "9", and "10". This results in 88+239+18 = 345 (19%+53%+4% = 76%).
